name: Asociacion De Puertorriquenos En Marcha
description_short: Philadelphia-based nonprofit providing adoption, foster care, early childhood education, and community development services with bilingual, culturally-sensitive staff.
description_long:
Asociación Puertorriqueños en Marcha (APM) is a longstanding community organization serving Philadelphia and surrounding counties since at least 2008. The organization operates as a comprehensive social services provider focused on family support, child welfare, and economic development with deep roots in the Puerto Rican and Latino communities.

APM offers a range of free and subsidized services including foster care and adoption services through its Pradera Corporation subsidiary, early childhood education programs, job support, benefit enrollment assistance, and community economic development initiatives. The organization currently manages 290 resource homes with 402 children in care and has secured adoptions for 244 children since 2008. Their early childhood education programs have served 610+ three- and four-year-olds, and they actively hire early childhood education professionals.

The organization distinguishes itself through bilingual and culturally-sensitive service delivery, with staff fluent in English and Spanish. Pradera received an "optimal" rating—the highest score available—on Philadelphia's Department of Human Services 2021 Foster Care Report. More than 25% of their resource homes are prepared to serve English-limited and primarily Spanish-speaking children. The caregiver and child populations they serve reflect the communities (44% African American, 37% Hispanic caregivers; 47% African American, 41% Hispanic children).

APM is a legitimate nonprofit community organization providing genuine social services rather than financial products. However, they do not offer credit counseling, debt relief, credit repair, or other financial consumer services. The organization's relevance to CreditDoc users is limited unless they are seeking family services, foster care, adoption, or early childhood education rather than financial assistance.
pros: Achieved "optimal" rating on Philadelphia DHS 2021 Foster Care Report—highest available score
Bilingual and culturally-sensitive staff prioritizing Spanish-speaking and English-limited populations
Trauma-informed, holistic approach to child welfare with focus on community preservation
244 children successfully placed through adoption since 2008
22% of resource homes certified for youth with specialized behavioral health needs
Contracted with Philadelphia, Montgomery, and Bucks Counties for foster care services
Free early childhood education and job support/benefit enrollment services
cons: Does not provide credit counseling, debt relief, or any financial consumer services
No financial hardship assistance, emergency loans, or alternative financial products
Limited scope for users seeking credit or debt-related help
Website contains unrelated content injection (casino and gaming spam) suggesting potential security issues
Not appropriate for consumer finance needs—purely social services organization
best_for: Philadelphia-area families seeking foster care or adoption services
Parents seeking affordable early childhood education programs
Spanish-speaking families needing culturally-sensitive child welfare support
diagnosis: APM is a legitimate nonprofit social services provider serving Philadelphia families, but it does not belong in a consumer finance database. This organization is exclusively focused on child welfare, foster care, adoption, and early childhood education—not financial products or credit services. Users seeking financial assistance, credit help, or debt solutions should not contact APM.
services: Foster care resource home services (290 homes, 402 children)
Adoption services and permanent legal custody support
Early childhood education programs for ages 3-4
Specialized behavioral health resource homes for youth with complex needs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
